concerns.text: Task T021 implements 'early stopping (2 epochs max)' which silently weakens Spec FR-003. FR-003 mandates training 'until validation loss plateaus for 3 consecutive epochs OR a maximum of 20 epochs'. The task removes the 'plateau' condition entirely, replacing a dynamic stopping criterion with a hard cap, violating the requirement's semantic intent.

Task T031 specifies performing the Wilcoxon signed-rank test on 'per-image validity scores across multiple seeds'. This is a semantic violation of Spec FR-006 and US-3. FR-006 requires the test to be performed 'across multiple random seeds' to determine significance of the performance gap. Aggregating per-image scores across seeds conflates the statistical unit (seed vs. image) and fails to satisfy the requirement for seed-level significance testing.

Task T005 implements a 'kill process on overflow' mechanism. Spec FR-007 requires a 'resource monitoring and enforcement mechanism' to 'enforce a maximum memory usage limit'. While killing is a form of enforcement, the spec's phrasing implies an active management strategy (throttling/guarding) to stay within limits, not just a reactive kill switch. The task description lacks the 'monitoring' aspect required to proactively enforce the limit, potentially violating the 'enforcement mechanism' requirement.

Task T033 logs 'total runtime' to metrics.json. Spec SC-005 requires measuring total time 'against the CI job time limit of a predefined duration'. The task logs the value but does not include the verification step to compare it against the 6-hour threshold defined in the plan/spec, leaving the success criterion unverified in the task deliverable.
